title: Listening - Part 3
description: For each question, write the correct answer in the gap. Write one or two words or a number or a date or a time.
instructions: You will hear one person giving information. For questions 14–19, write the correct answer in the gap.
context: You will hear a woman giving information about a beach clean-up.
audio_script: Speaker: Thanks for offering to help with this year's beach clean-up. The event is on Sunday morning and is part of a national project to protect the coast. Our local organiser is Jack [14]Turner[/14]. He will be wearing a bright green jacket, so he should be easy to find. Please meet him at [15]quarter past nine[/15] near the steps down to the beach. The clean-up begins at half past nine, but we need time to give out equipment. We will provide gloves and bags, but everyone should bring a bottle of [16]water[/16], especially if the weather is warm. Volunteers will work in small groups, and the first area to clean is around the [17]rock pools[/17], where plastic often gets caught after high tide. Please do not pick up anything sharp; call a group leader instead. Children under twelve must stay with an adult while they are on the beach. After the clean-up, there will be free sandwiches in the [18]sailing club[/18] for all volunteers. The event usually finishes at about one o'clock. Last year, our volunteers collected [19]seventy[/19] bags of rubbish, and we hope to do even better this time. Please remember to sign in before you start, so we know who is on the beach. The beach is easy to reach by bus, and there is a small car park beside the sea wall. Please check the weather before you come and wear shoes that can get wet. If the wind is very strong, we may move the clean-up to the path above the beach.
